title: trifolium physodes
intro: Trifolium physodes, also known as the small bird's-foot trefoil, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Western Asia. It is a flowering plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae) and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Trifolium physodes include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Trifolium physodes can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and wounds.
